body.title: [sprint] Flip survivor skills to single-source (project-scope .claude/skills)
body.intention: I intend that each managed skill has exactly one editable copy (the repo), so drift cannot recur.
body.description:
**Problem:** two copies still exist; flip survivors to single-source so drift can't recur. Merge-before-flip is the load-bearing invariant (gated behind the content-merge ticket).

**Shape:** CHOSEN mechanism = project-scope `.claude/skills/` — a committed in-repo symlink `<repo>/.claude/skills -> skills` so CC auto-loads canonical skills when working in the repo (verified: loader follows symlinks; project scope is a real load path). Per-skill ~/.claude/skills/<name> -> $UU_ROOT/skills/<name> symlinks are the FALLBACK if project-scope precedence is wrong. Clear the personal (~/.claude/skills) copies of MANAGED skills so they can't shadow the repo (confirm project-vs-personal precedence first). Two-tier rule: canonical = repo (symlink/project-scope); local-only = real dir in ~/.claude/skills (Igor-runtime: diagnose/debug/dream/igor-diagnose). Update skills/skills-sync/run: deploy = 'ensure symlink / leave local-only alone', drop the destructive copy-overwrite. NOTE: a newly-created skill symlink needs a CC restart to be watched.

**Affected files:** repo .claude/skills symlink (new); ~/.claude/skills/ (remove managed copies); skills/manifest.json (semantics: symlink not copy); skills/skills-sync/run.
**Design rules:** single-source; two-tier (canonical=repo / local-only=Igor); UU_ROOT-resolved; portable across machines.
**Scope boundary:** IN — flip managed survivors to project-scope single-source + update skills-sync + restart note + clear shadowing personal copies. OUT — skill content (done prior); Igor-local skills (untouched).
**Test plan:** after flip + CC restart, editing a repo SKILL.md changes the loaded skill; ~/.claude/skills has no managed-skill copies (only Igor-local real dirs); Igor-local skills still load; the drift audit (comm + per-skill diff) returns zero.
**Completion criteria:** exactly one editable copy of each managed skill (the repo); drift audit returns zero; Igor-local skills unaffected; skills-sync deploys via symlink, not destructive copy; project-vs-personal precedence confirmed so no stale personal copy shadows.

## Akien 2026-06-24 — fork #1 resolution (OVERRIDES the project-scope-primary choice above)
CHOSEN mechanism = PER-SKILL symlinks: for each repo skill, link `~/.claude/skills/<name> -> $UU_ROOT/skills/<name>`. NOT a folder link. Local-only skills (no repo counterpart) are left UNTOUCHED (coexist). A real (non-symlink) dir colliding with a repo-skill name -> drop a system_alarm (operations/system_alarms/, canonical name system_alarm) and ASK the user — the ONLY non-scriptable step. Akien has ZERO local skills, so for him the flip is clean. NO two-way sync — sync was the drift engine; links only. (Project-scope <repo>/.claude/skills symlink may ALSO be added for in-repo auto-load, but the per-skill global links are the chosen primary.)

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
